title: viola novae-angliae
intro: Viola novae-angliae, also known as the New England violet or woodland violet, is a perennial flowering plant belonging to the violet family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various skin conditions and wounds due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The New England violet has been shown to have numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, salves, and infusions, allowing for a range of uses in traditional medicine.
